args.answer: The Roman Empire (27 BCE–395 CE) centered in Rome and founded by Augustus, while the Holy Roman Empire (800–1806 CE) was a Germanic-centered polity founded by Charlemagne. The Roman Empire spanned the Mediterranean with Latin/Greek culture, whereas the Holy Roman Empire was a fragmented Germanic-French alliance under papal influence. Roman Empire capitals included Rome; Holy Roman Empire used Aachen/Vienna. The Holy Roman Empire was neither holy, nor Roman, nor an empire per Voltaire.
